strengths: Strong distributed-systems background: led low-latency microservices handling millions of HTTP requests/sec at Dunnhumby; demonstrates ability to architect and operate at scale
Polyglot engineer with proven capacity to master diverse tech stacks (TypeScript, DLang, C++, Swift, Java) across domains; currently building AI-agent infrastructure at re:cinq
17+ year career with technical depth across full-stack development, DevOps infrastructure, and open-source contributions (GISCollective, Trial, Fluent-Asserts)
concerns: No demonstrated production experience building or operating multi-agent AI systems; current agentic-AI work at re:cinq spans only 2 months—far below the 5+ years required for CTO-level architectural ownership

Career shows senior/staff individual-contributor roles with no clear evidence of executive leadership: no mention of scaling teams from 10→30+ people, hiring/developing technical leaders, or organizational impact at venture-backed scale-up pace
No fintech-infrastructure domain knowledge (credit systems, payment flows, working-capital management, regulated AI systems); RIDE Capital was investment-adjacent but not fintech infrastructure

key_factors.factor: Executive team scaling and technical-leadership track record

response.justification: Szabo is an exceptional senior engineer with genuine distributed-systems depth and recent agentic-AI exposure. However, he falls substantially short of CTO requirements: no documented production experience with multi-agent AI systems, no evidence of executive-level team building or organizational scaling (the role explicitly requires developing senior technical leaders and scaling from 10→30+ people), and no fintech background. His resume reflects a gifted individual contributor/architect rather than an executive leader. He would be a strong VP Engineering or Principal Engineer candidate after building executive-level experience.
